// SETUP: This is a template. Copy or merge into your .claude/settings.json.
Replace every [FILL-IN] placeholder with project-specific content.
Hooks fire automatically — no manual invocation needed per turn.
All 4 hook types are generalizable; customize content per your stack.
The additionalContext string is injected into Claude's context before
it responds — use it to reinforce rules that drift in long sessions.

hooks.command: echo '{"additionalContext": "[HOOK after Edit/Write] PRE-COMMIT CHECKLIST: (a) [FILL-IN: test command — e.g. npm test -- --run] ALL PASS (b) [FILL-IN: build command — e.g. npm run build] clean — catches Edit silent-fail that tests miss (c) run area audit skill matching files touched (d) GREP-PAIR: every router case has a matching handler def, count must match (e) trace at least one real caller for mutation paths. [EDIT-TOOL SILENT-FAIL TRAP] A parameter typo causes the Edit tool to error silently — always grep the expected text to confirm it landed. See .claude/rules/02-workflow.md for full checklist."}'

PostToolUse.// WHY: V6 anti-example: agent called Edit with a param typo, function body never landed, claimed committed. Runtime crash in production. This hook injects the pre-commit checklist after every file write so it can not be skipped.

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "[FILL-IN: deploy command pattern — e.g. vercel --prod]"; then if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "[FILL-IN: areas that are commit-only, never deployed — e.g. (backend|Backend|admin-only)]"; then echo "BLOCKED: [FILL-IN: reason message — e.g. Backend files are commit-only. Use git push only. vercel --prod = Vercel cost for files that run locally.]." >&2; exit 2; fi; fi; exit 0'
hooks.// WHY: Prevents deploying file areas that should only be committed (e.g. backend-only tabs, dev-only scaffolding, admin-only dashboards). Two FILL-IN patterns: first detects deploy command, second detects the blocked area.

hooks.command: echo '{"additionalContext": "[MANDATORY FIRST READ: .claude/rules/00-session-start.md — character + expectations + iron-clad rules A-H + past violations V1-Vn + pre-commit checklist. READ BEFORE ANY TOOL CALL.] [IRON-CLAD SUMMARY] A: bug → revert, never patch forward. B: [FILL-IN: deploy safety probe — e.g. probe unauth write paths before + after firestore rules deploy]. C: Rule of 3 / crypto.getRandomValues for tokens / no uid in public docs / no allow:if-true without comment. D: every bug → adversarial test + rule update + audit invariant. E: [FILL-IN: data-boundary rule — e.g. backend UI reads our DB only]. F: Triangle Rule — capture external ref + plan + existing code before writing. G: load deferred tools/skills as needed — rules still apply. H: [FILL-IN: data-ownership rule]. [DEPLOY] Requires explicit THIS-TURN authorization — prior auth does not roll over to next commit or next turn."}'

SessionStart.// WHY: Without a SessionStart hook, the agent starts cold with no awareness of the iron-clad rules. The hook injects a mandatory-read reminder even if the user forgets to invoke /session-start manually.

hooks.command: echo '{"additionalContext": "[IRON-CLAD PRE-PROMPT — every turn] A: bug → revert. B: [FILL-IN: deploy safety short form]. C: Rule of 3 / crypto tokens / lean schema / no uid leaks. D: bug → test + rule + audit invariant. E: [FILL-IN: data boundary short form]. F: Triangle — capture before guessing URL/method/field. G: load tools/skills on demand (no ask). Deploy = new explicit ask every turn. Full rules: .claude/rules/00-session-start.md."}'

UserPromptSubmit.// WHY: Iron-clad rules drift in long conversations. Injecting a summary every prompt keeps the 8 rules in active context without the agent having to re-read the rule files each turn. Pair with rules/00-session-start.md for the full text.
